name: Para (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the two red areas recall the blood shed by the Cabanos in the Cabanagem revolt
the white band represents the Equator, the Amazon River, and the zodiac
the blue star Spica (Alpha Virginis), representing Para

description: A red field crossed by an oblique white band running from the upper hoist to the lower fly, charged at the centre with a single blue five-pointed star.
history: Adopted in 1889 from the badge of the Clube Republicano Paraense, just after the Proclamation of the Republic; the single star is Spica, the same star that represents Para in the celestial sphere of the Brazilian national flag.

features.symbolism: an oblique white band on a red field, running from the upper hoist to the lower fly; the white band represents the Equator, the Amazon and the zodiac, the red fields the blood of the Cabanagem revolt
Spica (Alpha Virginis) in Virgo, the star representing Para on the Brazilian national flag
